Title: Discovering a mitochondrion-localized BAHD acyltransferase involved in calystegine biosynthesis and engineering the production of 3β-tigloyloxytropane

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-specific/root-preferring; expressed in primary and secondary roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In A. belladonna, highest in primary and secondary roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: A. belladonna primary and secondary roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: 3β-tigloyloxytropane is a potential drug for treating neurodegenerative disease; calystegines inhibit glycosidases and are anti-nutritional factors that may affect nutrient absorption and may have applications in therapy of metabolic disorders
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes formation of 3β-tigloyloxytropane from 3β-tropanol and tigloyl-CoA; silencing reduces 3β-tigloyloxytropane and downstream calystegine A3, while overexpression increases 3β-tigloyloxytropane accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metabolite-gene association analysis; qPCR; recombinant enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS product identification; VIGS silencing in A. belladonna; overexpression in hairy roots; subcellular localization in tobacco protoplasts; mitochondrial fraction enzyme assay; transient expression in N. benthamiana; heterologous production in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TS (aba locus 5896) was identified as a root-expressed BAHD acyltransferase tightly associated with 3β-tigloyloxytropane distribution in A. belladonna. 2. TS catalyzes esterification of 3β-tropanol with tigloyl-CoA to form 3β-tigloyloxytropane and shows highest catalytic efficiency with tigloyl-CoA over acetyl-CoA and benzoyl-CoA. 3. TS is unusually localized to mitochondria rather than cytosol. 4. TS silencing decreases 3β-tigloyloxytropane, tigloyl norpseudotropine, tigloyl 1-hydroxynorpseudotropine, and calystegine A3, and increases upstream 3β-tropanol-related metabolites. 5. TS overexpression raises 3β-tigloyloxytropane in hairy roots. 6. TS enabled pathway reconstruction in tobacco and, with PcICS, semi-biosynthesis in E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a tigloyl group from tigloyl-CoA to 3β-tropanol to form 3β-tigloyloxytropane via esterification; can also use acetyl-CoA and benzoyl-CoA weakly to form corresponding esters
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and key intermediate for calystegine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream branch step; committed esterification step in 3β-tropane alkaloid branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing esterification step in calystegine biosynthesis; bottleneck/control step supported but not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro optimum pH 8.6 and 30°C; transient expression in tobacco leaves; substrate feeding with 3β-tropanol and tiglic acid for engineering
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pBI121 CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in hairy root overexpression; pEAQ-HT transient expression vectors in tobacco; pETDuet-1 in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome-based HMMER search and metabolite-gene association analysis; no public accession reported for transcriptome dataset beyond Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for breeding calystegine-free Solanaceae crops; useful for metabolic engineering of 3β-tigloyloxytropane and calystegine-related alkaloids in plants and microbes; mitochondrial localization may be exploitable for BAHD-mediated ester production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike typical cytosolic BAHD acyltransferases, TS is mitochondrion-localized. The N-terminal 32 aa contribute strongly to activity. TS also forms 3β-acetoxytropane and trace 3β-benzoyloxytropane, showing donor promiscuity but strong preference for tigloyl-CoA. TS does not use 3α-tropanol as acceptor.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana and E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Engineered tobacco leaves; engineered E. coli culture
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential drug for treating neurodegenerative disease
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves catalytic efficiency and boosts engineered production of 3β-tigloyloxytropane relative to wild-type TS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Consensus protein design; site-directed mutagenesis; enzyme kinetics; transient expression in N. benthamiana; heterologous fermentation in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. S40T mutation increased TS catalytic activity without major Km changes. 2. In tobacco leaves, TSS40T increased 3β-tigloyloxytropane and 3β-acetoxytropane over wild-type TS. 3. In E. coli coexpressed with PcICS, TSS40T enabled the highest 3β-tigloyloxytropane yield (357.40 mg/L, 90.9% conversion).
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Enhanced esterification of 3β-tropanol with tigloyl-CoA compared with wild-type TS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tobacco leaf transient expression; E. coli fermentation with fed 3β-tropanol and tiglic acid
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vectors in tobacco; pETDuet-1 in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred engineered TS allele for synthetic biology production of 3β-tigloyloxytropane
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Thr40 forms a new hydrogen bond with Asn135, likely stabilizing the active center and improving activity.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana and E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Engineered tobacco leaves; engineered E. coli culture
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential drug for treating neurodegenerative disease
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides tigloyl-CoA from tiglic acid and CoA, enabling and enhancing engineered 3β-tigloyloxytropane production with TS/TSS40T
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS detection of tigloyl-CoA; coexpression in tobacco; coexpression in E. coli fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcICS converts tiglic acid and CoA to tigloyl-CoA in vitro. 2. PcICS supplies the acyl donor required for TS-mediated 3β-tigloyloxytropane formation. 3. Coexpression of PcICS with TSS40T in E. coli enabled high-titer semi-biosynthesis of 3β-tigloyloxytropane.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Ligates CoA to tiglic acid to form tigloyl-CoA, the acyl donor used by TS
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key donor-supply step for heterologous production in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding of tiglic acid and 3β-tropanol in tobacco leaves and E. coli cultures
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vectors in tobacco; pETDuet-1 in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful donor-supply module for microbial or plant engineering of tigloyl esters and CoA-recycling systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a native plant calystegine gene; included because it is a core experimentally validated enzyme enabling target metabolite production in applied heterologous systems.
